xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: 616, Tulsiani Chambers Nariman Point, Mumbai City, Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, 400021
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Office No. 601, Part B-2, Metro Tower, 6th Floor, Vijay Nagar, AB Road, Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India, 452010
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesignationOfAssessorOrAssurer: Technical reviewer- ESG & Sustainibility Services, SGS INdia
Lead Verifier -ESG & Sustainability Services, SGS India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfNumberOfD: Indicators that were verified: (a) Number of days of accounts payable
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfDetailsOf: Indicators that were verified: (a) Concentration of purchases & sales done with trading houses, dealers, and related parties, (b) Loans and advances & investments with related parties
Indicators that were verified: (a) Spending on measures towards well-being of employees as a % of total revenue of the Company
Indicators that were verified: (a) Details of safety related incidents for employees
Indicators that were verified: (a) Gross wages paid to females as % of wages paid
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfComplaint: Indicators that were verified: (a) Complaints on POSH
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfDetailsOf: Indicators that were verified: (a) Total energy consumed, (b) % of energy consumed from renewable sources – Energy intensity
Indiactors that were verified: (a) Total water consumption, (b) Water consumption intensity
Indiactors that were verified: (a) Water Discharge by destination and levels of Treatment
Indicators that were verified: (a) Total Scope 1 emissions, (b) Total Scope 2 emissions, (c) GHG Emission Intensity (Scope 1 +2)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfDetailsRe: Indicators that were verified: (a) Plastic waste, (b) E-waste, (c) Bio-Medical Waste, (d) Construction and Demolition waste, (e) Battery waste, (f) Other hazardous waste, (g) Other non-hazardous waste, (h) Total waste generated, (i) Waste intensity, (j) Total waste recovered through recycling, re-using or other recovery operations, (k) Total waste disposed by nature of disposal method
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfPercentag: Indicators that were verified: (a) Input material sourced from MSMEs/ small producers as % of total purchases
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfJobCreati: Indicators that were verified: (a) Job creation in smaller towns - Wages paid to persons
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfDataBreac: Indicators that were verified: (a) Instances involving loss/breach of data of customers as a percentage of total data breaches or cyber security events
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Edible Oils, Food & Other FMCG, Home and Personal Care (Food and Beverages, etc)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Manufacture of vegetable oils and fats excluding corn oil
Wholesale of Edible Oils fats sugar and processed/manufactured spices etc
Manufacture of biscuits, cakes, pastries, rusks etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: We are a leading player in the Food & FMCG, Home and Personal Care, and Edible Oils sectors, offering products that serve premium to mass market segments across urban and rural India. We cater to a diverse customer base, including institutional buyers and the HoReCa sector. Our products are also exported to 37 countries, reinforcing our growing global presence. To effectively serve this wide customer base, we have rolled out multiple retail formats to address the evolving needs of discerning consumers. We have established a robust distribution network comprising over 8,000 distributors, 98 sales depots, 2 million+ retail touchpoints, complemented by 1,039 Chikitsalays, 387 Mega Stores, 3,420 Aarogya Kendras, and Grameen Aarogya Kendras, ensuring convenient availability of our products. Our portfolio is accessible through e-commerce platforms and organized retail channels, enhancing market reach. Our “Order Me” app enhances online customer engagement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: No complaints have been reported by the communities in which we operate
The complaints were duly resolved during the financial year to the satisfaction of shareholders
The complaints were duly resolved during the financial year to the satisfaction of shareholders
The complaints were duly resolved during the financial year to the satisfaction of shareholders

Complaints received from customers were promptly addressed, and corrective measures were implemented to improve service quality. The outstanding complaints received in normal course are being addressed by respective departments in accordance with Grievance Redressal Mechanism of the Company.

All customer complaints were recorded, reviewed, and resolved within defined timelines, with feedback used to strengthen product and service standards. The outstanding complaints were closed at the last financial year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Supply chain disruptions and commodity price inflation may impact the availability and cost of raw materials, particularly due to our dependence on both domestic and international sourcing. We aim to reduce import dependence by promoting domestic production of palm oil while simultaneously expanding our presence in international markets, enhancing supply chain resilience and supporting sustainable growth.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: To address this risk, we are focusing on reducing import dependence by promoting domestic production of key raw materials. This includes capitalizing on domestic oil palm expansion programs to reduce import dependency and aggressively increasing the oil palm planted area. Additionally, we are enhancing supply chain resilience through strategic sourcing and fostering strong relationships with local suppliers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Slowdown in economic activity could impact the FMCG industry and sales. Volatility in interest rates may affect financial performance and lead to investment losses.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Align product portfolio with a mix of premium, mass, and economy segment products, manage interest rate risk, and maintain adequate liquidity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Foreign currency fluctuations and commodity price changes impact financial performance due to import dependency. Business disruption from geopolitical events may occur. The Company aims to reduce import dependence through domestic production l. This initiative aligns with changing consumer preferences and global demand for sustainable products.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Minimize import dependency, expand domestic oil palm plantation, and increase exports to create a natural foreign currency hedge.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Enhancing corporate governance improves business transformation and reputation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Establish and implement internal policies and improve compliances.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Tracking consumer trends and strengthening dealer networks improve brand reach and cater to changing preferences.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Constantly analyse the market, selecting the best suppliers and venture into promising markets.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Providing access to healthy products addresses malnourishment and promotes well-being. Integrating plantbased products enhances the portfolio
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Enhance distribution network, create affordable healthy products meeting preferences
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Transitioning to circular models with reduced reliance on non-renewable resources creates a sustainable business model.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Improve the renewable energy mix and focus on reusing and recycling materials wherever possible across the value creation model.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Climate change events impact business. Opportunity lies in sourcing sustainability and developing local supply chains.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Sustainable sourcing, water-saving solutions, local supply chains, and oil palm plantation in India.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Non-compliance with laws damages reputation and affects business growth. Adverse changes in regulations could impact results.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Comply with State & Central laws, monitor and review compliance, and maintain vigil mechanisms.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Disruptions from cyberattacks and non-availability of critical information impact business operations and results.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Robust IT systems, firewalls, contingency measures, and employee training.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Talent acquisition, retention, and employee health, safety & well-being contribute to sustainable business success.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Create an inclusive and highperforming work environment, encourage entrepreneurship, reward performance and prioritise employee safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ensuring production quality and safety enhance brand reputation amid increasing transparency requirements.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Obtain third-party certifications to ensure product safety and quality.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Focus on waste, water, and energy management along with GHG emissions reduction.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Use renewable energy, adopt eco-friendly practices (5 R principle).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Product contamination can lead to recalls, legal issues, and reputational damage.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implement stringent food safety protocols, regular quality audits, traceability, and staff training.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: Our manufacturing units are covered by a Comprehensive Quality Management System which includes: QMS-9001:2015 EMS-14001-2015 OHSAS-45001-2018 FSSC- 22000- 2018 SEDEX GMP+ Feed Safety System Food chain ID (formerly known as Cert id) Halal Certification Kosher FSSAI HACCP – Food Safety RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) GFSI-recognized standards: BRC, FSSC Non-GMO Certification GMP+ (Good Manufacturing Practices Plus) Compliance with BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards), Agmark, APEDA, USFDA

Our manufacturing units are covered by a Comprehensive Quality Management System which includes: QMS-9001:2015 EMS-14001-2015 OHSAS-45001-2018 FSSC- 22000- 2018 SEDEX GMP+ Feed Safety System Food chain ID (formerly known as Cert id) Halal Certification Kosher FSSAI HACCP – Food Safety RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) GFSI-recognized standards: BRC, FSSC Non-GMO Certification GMP+ (Good Manufacturing Practices Plus) Compliance with BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards), Agmark, APEDA, USFDA

Our manufacturing units are covered by a Comprehensive Quality Management System which includes: QMS-9001:2015 EMS-14001-2015 OHSAS-45001-2018 FSSC- 22000- 2018 SEDEX GMP+ Feed Safety System Food chain ID (formerly known as Cert id) Halal Certification Kosher FSSAI HACCP – Food Safety RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) GFSI-recognized standards: BRC, FSSC Non-GMO Certification GMP+ (Good Manufacturing Practices Plus) Compliance with BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards), Agmark, APEDA, USFDA

Our manufacturing units are covered by a Comprehensive Quality Management System which includes: QMS-9001:2015 EMS-14001-2015 OHSAS-45001-2018 FSSC- 22000- 2018 SEDEX GMP+ Feed Safety System Food chain ID (formerly known as Cert id) Halal Certification Kosher FSSAI HACCP – Food Safety RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) GFSI-recognized standards: BRC, FSSC Non-GMO Certification GMP+ (Good Manufacturing Practices Plus) Compliance with BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards), Agmark, APEDA, USFDA

Our manufacturing units are covered by a Comprehensive Quality Management System which includes: QMS-9001:2015 EMS-14001-2015 OHSAS-45001-2018 FSSC- 22000- 2018 SEDEX GMP+ Feed Safety System Food chain ID (formerly known as Cert id) Halal Certification Kosher FSSAI HACCP – Food Safety RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) GFSI-recognized standards: BRC, FSSC Non-GMO Certification GMP+ (Good Manufacturing Practices Plus) Compliance with BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards), Agmark, APEDA, USFDA

Our manufacturing units are covered by a Comprehensive Quality Management System which includes: QMS-9001:2015 EMS-14001-2015 OHSAS-45001-2018 FSSC- 22000- 2018 SEDEX GMP+ Feed Safety System Food chain ID (formerly known as Cert id) Halal Certification Kosher FSSAI HACCP – Food Safety RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil) GFSI-recognized standards: BRC, FSSC Non-GMO Certification GMP+ (Good Manufacturing Practices Plus) Compliance with BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards), Agmark, APEDA, USFDA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: In November 2024, Patanjali Foods Ltd. acquired the Home & Personal Care (HPC) business through a slump sale, expanding our portfolio and reinforcing our commitment to embed sustainability at the core of our strategy. We set short-, medium-, and long-term internal targets, reviewing progress periodically to drive continuous improvement
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT: Key Sustainability Highlights (FY 2024-25): • Use of Green Energy & Emissions Reduction: Generated 84.6 MW of wind energy (22% for captive use), commissioned the Bhagwanpura solar plant at full scale, and expanded biomass usage including rice husk, mustard husk, and palm fiber as boiler fuel. Achieved a 23% increase in renewable fuel consumption (Energy basis), 7% reduction in non-renewable fuel use, 7% reduction in Scope 1 & 2 GHG emissions, and Sequestered 87,336 tCO2 through wind projects. • Agriculture & Water Management: Expanded oil palm plantations to 89,546 ha under drip irrigation, Our Oil Palm Plantation business positively impacted 63,915 farmers—an increase from over 50,000 in FY24 - through direct procurement of Fresh Fruit Bunches for Crude Palm Oil processing Supported oil palm plantation expansion through operation of 49 nurseries, 21 fertilizer godowns & 173 farmer information cum FFB collection centres across 12 states. Allocated 202 Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) to empower and support the agricultural community. • Community Development: Created 6.77% of jobs in rural India and invested H21.87 crore in CSR programs focused on health, education, and sports. • Environmental Stewardship: Increased ZLD plants to 7 sites, with advanced effluent treatment (MEE/MVR/ATFD) at all other facilities, and promoted organically grown products in our nutraceutical portfolio. These accomplishments reflect our enduring commitment to sustainability, operational excellence, and a positive impact on both environment and society.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: I am pleased to present our Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) for the financial year 2024–25, during which we successfully completed the acquisition of the Home & Personal Care (HPC) Business. This report reflects our commitment to responsible and sustainable business practices. We work continuously to embed sustainability into the core of our operations. We set short, medium, and long-term targets and review progress periodically to ensure steady advancement toward our sustainability goals. In FY 2024–25, we revisited these targets and achieved several milestones: Renewable energy: We generated 84.6 MW of wind energy, of which 18.6 MW (22%) was used for our own operations. We also completed the first year of operations at our Bhagwanpura solar power plant. Renewable energy now contributes 22% of our operations, supplemented by biomass as renewable fuel across several plants. Collectively, our green energy initiatives led to the sequestration of 87,336 tonnes of CO2. Energy efficiency: Targeted measures increased renewable fuel consumption by 23%, reduced non-renewable fuel consumption by 7%, and lowered Scope 1 and Scope 2 GHG emissions by 7% compared to the previous year. Oil palm expansion: Our oil palm plantations now span 89,546 hectares, all equipped with drip irrigation systems. We directly procured Fresh Fruit Bunches (FFBs) from 63,915 farmers. Supporting infrastructure includes 49 nurseries, 21 fertilizer godowns, 173 collection centres, and partnerships with 202 Farmer Producer Organizations. Community development: Rural India accounted for 6.77% of total jobs created. We invested H21.87 crore in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives focused on health, education, and sports. Environmental stewardship: We expanded Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) facilities to seven sites and ensured all other locations operate advanced effluent treatment systems. Our nutraceutical business continues to promote organically grown products. These achievements highlight our commitment to sustainability, operational excellence, and creating long-term value for the environment and society. For further details, please refer to the “MD’s Message” section on Page 50 of this Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: To further strengthen our governance on sustainability and corporate social responsibility, we have constituted an ESG & CSR Committee (ECC). The ECC is responsible for steering all sustainability and CSR-related decisions and is chaired by Acharya Balkrishna, Non- Executive Director, providing strong leadership and dedicated oversight to advance our ESG commitments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfOtherFrequencyFor: On a periodic basis, the policies are reviewed internally.
On a periodic basis, the policies are reviewed internally.
On a periodic basis, the policies are reviewed internally.

On a periodic basis, the policies are reviewed internally.
On a periodic basis, the policies are reviewed internally.

The Company is in compliance with applicable statutory requirements and monitors the same through a compliance tool implemented across its various facilities to ensure timely compliance. The tool is equipped with automated escalation matrix features to ensure prompt redressal of any issues.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Corporate Law and Governance Internal Financial Controls and Risk Management Hedging Operations on Commodity and Forex Compliance and Other Aspects
SEBI Insider Trading Compliance - Structural Digital Database and updated Code of Conduct under SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015. Internal Controls, Governance & Risk Oversight

Regulatory Compliance Trainings - SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015; Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH). Operational & Safety Trainings - Cybersecurity, ISO 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety), 5S methodology, FSSC 22000 (Food Safety). Team Development - Team Building and Team Development sessions. Job-Specific Trainings - Role-based and function-specific skill enhancement programs.
Electrical Safety Health & Safety Practices Quality Concepts Road & Fire Safety Lock-out-Tag-out (LOTO) System Use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Emergency Exit Planning
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Yes, we continue to maintain a Board-approved anti-corruption and anti-bribery policy. This policy underscores our commitment to ethical business practices and zero tolerance for corruption or bribery.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: Development and implementation of advanced utility generation system to make manufacturing process efficient and has procured indigenous technology of Co-generation (Steam Turbine & Generator set).

Energy efficiency improvement initiatives. Expansion of Oil Palm plantations area with drip irrigation facilities Supported farmers by purchasing 100% Fresh fruit bunches (FFB’s) produced by them. Commissioned modern Oil Palm Mill in Niglok – Arunachal Pradesh with efficiency gains, quality and yield improvement and sustainable production practices. Installed ZLD (Zero Liquid Discharge) facility.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: We are registered under Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR). In the reporting year, we have processed 100% of plastic waste.
Collected and managed through government-approved recyclers across India in compliance with regulatory requirements.
Treated in-house at Effluent Treatment Facilities or safely handed over to authorized handlers, strictly following prescribed norms.

All materials and manufacturing processes are designed to minimize waste generation. We follow the five R’s of waste management – Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, Recover, and Residual management, to ensure resource efficiency and responsible disposal of any remaining waste.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: This policy is publicly accessible for transparency and reference at the company’s official website here: https://www.patanjalifoods.com/brsr-policies/
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes. All our locations have an occupational health and safety management system in place. Through a comprehensive Environment, Health & Safety policy, the company lays down its commitment to safe and healthy working conditions. The policy is available on the company web site www.patanjalifoods.com. We have implemented a robust Occupational Health and Safety Management System that spans across all our operational units and offices. Our plants are ISO 45001 certified which covers the Health and Safety hazards with a commitment to continually improve our health and safety performance and prevention of injuries. We remain dedicated to conducting operations responsibly to safeguard our employees, the environment, and the communities in which we operate.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: We have adopted a comprehensive approach to identify work-related hazards and assess risks in order to maintain a safe working environment. Our multifaceted process incorporates periodic safety audits, inspections, and Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) techniques. Our advanced safety management system allows us to identify potential workplace risks and hazards, enabling us to implement preventive measures and avoid incidents. One of our key tools in maintaining safety is our near-miss reporting. This encourages employees to report any potential incidents within our premises, allowing us to conduct thorough investigations, determine causes, and implement corrective actions. Additionally, we have Environmental, Health, and Safety Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) in place to guide our operations and ensure the safety of our employees and workers. The primary goal of this control is to create a safe workplace free from harm, as well as to provide training and an awareness program on important workplace risks by using the techniques listed below: 1. Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) is used for routine and non-routine activities, conducted yearly or as required by any process change. 2. Safety Audits are carried out periodically to identify hazards and the corresponding risks. 3. Hazard and Operability Study (HAZOP) is used for identifying hazards related to chemical processes. Furthermore, we have a comprehensive Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS) Policy. The health and safety guidelines are applicable and followed across all operations. Some of the key processes for identifying work-related hazards and assessing risks on a routine and non-routine basis are given below: Pre-start safety review (PSSR) is used and followed for new processes, post any changes/modification activities and Plant restart up after preventive maintenance. The online work permit system implemented along with comprehensive JSEA to control non-routine job hazards and implement control measures against identified risk. Management of change (MOC) is implemented and followed for all changes including Man, Machine, and Material. Apart from the above-mentioned measures, to promote effective implementation of control measures, workers are provided with regular ‘Safety talks & Tool Box Talk’ to communicate available control measures and ensure that all safety protocols are being followed. Through these consistent efforts, we achieved a significant 70% reduction in Lost Time Injury (LTI) accidents across group companies, with zero fatal accidents recorded.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: We have established robust processes to identify, assess, and manage health and safety hazards across all work sites. Key practices include: 1. Online platform (EHS Software). 2. Online work permit system, Incident Investigation & CAPA tracker and Safety Observation and Scheduling has been implemented in all units/Plants . This enables us to track and monitor the EHS performance. 3. Monthly safety visits (Safety Walk) by operations team across all the sites and scheduled Audits and Inspections to ensure effective implementation of Safety Management systems. 4. All actionable points are tracked through Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) /Corrective Action Report for closure and reviewed frequently through online EHS software. 5. Best practices are benchmarked and shared across the business as part of continual improvement. 6. Safety/EHS Alerts are shared with all operational units as part of horizontal deployment and cross-site learning. 7. Fortnightly Safety Musing is shared with all employees. 8. We have integrated a behaviour-based Online Safety Observation Rounds module into our system, enabling online reporting of safe and unsafe practices, conditions and close calls in the workplace. The reported incidents and corrective actions taken are reviewed to determine corresponding remedial measures. 9. We also employ a structured Kaizen scheme that encourages employees at all levels to contribute safety-related suggestions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: The company utilizes a well-defined framework for investigating incidents. When incidents occur, investigations are triggered, followed by root cause analysis. Response strategies are designed and shared across all facilities to mitigate the impact. Additionally, the company has a system in place for sharing information regarding significant incidents that have occurred within group companies to facilitate learning and prevent recurrence. Initial incident investigation reports are uploaded to the company’s internal EHS software within 24 hours, in line with our safety policy guidelines. In the case of major incidents, cross-functional teams are deployed to conduct in-depth investigations to find the root cause. We have a robust audit system that facilitates internal and external audits for Environmental and Occupational Health Safety parameters throughout the year. Significant risks/concerns arising from assessment of Health and Safety practices are addressed through elimination of manual job by use of technology/digitization, safety capability building, monitoring and supervision. Some of the preventive measures and predictive actions implemented during the reporting period are given below: Preventive Measures: 1. Departmental ‘On the Job’ training for safety awareness. 2. Daily Toolbox talks in all departments. 3. Awareness through classroom training. 4. Periodic fire drills & mock drills. 5. Effective implementation of safety PPE matrix. 6. Strict safety compliances for critical activities and projects execution work.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
We view our stakeholders as integral partners in shaping our strategy and driving sustainable growth. We identify stakeholders based on their relationship and proximity to our operations, their dependence on our business, their ability to influence decisions, and our financial, legal, or ethical responsibilities toward them.
At the core are employees, farmers, suppliers, and customers, who are directly engaged in our value chain. At the next level are investors, governments bodies, and local communities who both influence and are impacted by our activities. In the wider ecosystem, we engage with industry bodies, NGOs, trade unions, and civil society organizations. We also recognize vulnerable groups, such as women, children, migrant workers, persons with disabilities, and Indigenous communities, who may be disproportionately affected and therefore require focused engagement. We engage through two-way, inclusive communication channels such as consultations, investor meetings and earnings calls, employee engagement programs, structured feedback processes, and grievance mechanisms. These efforts help us better understand expectations, address concerns, and proactively resolve issues that may impact long-term value creation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Customer feedback surveys Focused Group Discussions, Digital Platforms, Market Research (TV commercials, newspaper ads and pamphlets are in the local language, where applicable) Emails/phone calls/meetings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Product quality, safety, and affordability Transparency in ingredients and sourcing Responsiveness of customer service Alignment with health, wellness, and Ayurveda-based lifestyles Grievances Resolution
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Supplier Meeting Feedback Mechanisms Emails/phone calls
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: One-on-one meetings to discuss service levels or other commercial aspects Interactions regarding quality of raw materials, technology benefits, safety, health, environmental and ethical compliance
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Regular meetings, emails/ Phone calls Training programs, Helplines Field visits,
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Fair pricing and timely procurement of produce Access to quality seeds, inputs, and sustainable farming practices Training on modern agricultural techniques and organic methods Support for crop diversification and productivity enhancement Information on government schemes, subsidies, and financial inclusion
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Employee engagement surveys Informative and upto- date employee communication channels Regular interactions with the C-suite Town halls and open forums One-on-one performance reviews Learning and development initiative
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Direct engagements with supervisors and business management Conferences and town hall meetings for two-way communication Induction and internal training programs for skill development Outbound exercises to build teamwork and collaboration Employee wellness campaigns to promote health and wellbeing
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Statutory and Legal compliance filings Direct Engagement Collaboration on schemes related to agriculture, food safety, and rural development Policy advocacy through industry associations and chambers of commerce
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Audits of manufacturing sites by regulatory authorities Reports and interactions aimed at confirming legislative and regulatory compliance policies and processes Involvement in government programmes aimed at uplifting communities and reduce edible oil import dependence
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Active participation in industry associations, councils, Attending conferences, trade fairs, and industry events
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: To collaborate on advancing sectorwide best practices, strengthen policy advocacy, and contribute to collective initiatives on sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Annual General Meetings (AGMs) Quarterly earnings calls and financial disclosures Investor presentations and analyst meets One-on-one meetings Regular updates through stock exchange filings and press releases
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Share strategic direction, growth plans, and market outlook Provide updates on governance practices and compliance Highlight risk management measures and opportunities Communicate progress on ESG and sustainability commitments
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: As part of our commitment to transparency and accountability, we have established strong internal mechanisms to address grievances related to human rights concerns. For employees and workers, we provide multiple accessible channels such as HR Konnect, the Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH) Committee, and dedicated email support, through which concerns can be raised directly or anonymously. In addition, our Code of Conduct and Whistleblower Policy enable individuals across the organization to report issues, including those linked to human rights, without fear of retaliation. Our grievance redressal framework extends beyond employees to include suppliers, farmers, local communities, and other stakeholders who may be affected by our operations. Dedicated feedback and grievance mechanisms are available at site level and through our community outreach programs, ensuring that concerns from external stakeholders are also heard and addressed. We place special emphasis on vulnerable groups, such as women, children, migrant workers, and marginalized communities. Mechanisms are designed to remain accessible, confidential, and culturally appropriate, ensuring these groups can raise issues without barriers. For example, grievances can be submitted in local languages, and communitylevel engagement channels provide safe spaces for vulnerable groups to express concerns. All grievances are formally recorded, reviewed, and investigated by the Human Resources team under the oversight of the Chief Operating Officer (COO). Outcomes and corrective actions are communicated back to the concerned parties, except in cases where anonymity is chosen. Over time, we plan to implement ISO 26000 on Social Responsibility, establish robust mechanisms to adopt a comprehensive policy to uphold the rights of all stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: We made a significant progress in sustainable water management by implementing Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) systems at seven locations, with plans underway to extend this initiative to additional sites. To minimize environmental impact and freshwater consumption: Advanced treatment technologies such as Multiple Effect Evaporators (MEE), Mechanical Vapor Recompression (MVR), Agitated Thin Film Dryers (ATFD), and Effluent Treatment Plants (ETPs) have been installed across all other manufacturing facilities. Recycled water is reused for horticulture and industrial purposes, lowering intake from natural freshwater sources. Solid residues generated through treatment processes are safely managed and disposed of via authorized agencies in line with environmental standards. We strictly adhere to all applicable environmental regulations and comply with the standards and directions issued by the Pollution Control Boards, reaffirming our commitment to sustainable operations and environmental stewardship.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Continuing our commitment to sustainable practices, we strictly adhere to government regulations for the safe and compliant disposal of hazardous waste. We are focused on minimizing waste generation and disposal by adopting the “5 R’s” principle: Refuse, Reduce, Reuse, Repurpose, and Recycle—wherever feasible across our operations. A variety of initiatives support this waste reduction strategy:In our Palm Plantation business, solid waste such as Empty Fruit Bunches (EFB), fibre, and shells is effectively utilized as organic fertilizer and fuel, promoting a closed-loop system. Liquid waste, including Palm Oil Mill Effluent (POME), is sustainably managed to minimize environmental impact. In the Oleo Chemicals business, byproducts from edible oil processing are used as primary feedstock, reinforcing our commitment to resource efficiency and circular economy practices. These integrated waste management practices help us to reduce our environmental footprint and promote long-term resource sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOrDetailsOfTheLawOrRegulat: Water (Prevention & Control of Pollution Act, 1974 by Karnataka State Pollution Control Board.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideDetailsOfTheNonComplian: Installation of the RO plant and commissioning of Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) plant not completed along with other aspects related to discharge.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AnyFinesOrPenaltiesOrActionTak: Company directed to submit its reply to KSPCB notices
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOrDetailsOfTheLawOrRegulat: Central Ground Water Authority (GWA), New Delhi
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideDetailsOfTheNonComplian: Non- fulfilment of previous NOC recharge criteria under Central Ground Water Regulations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AnyFinesOrPenaltiesOrActionTak: CGWA has imposed penalty amount of ₹ 1,00,000/- (Rupee One Lakh only)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CorrectiveActionTakenForNonCom: All compliances as required has been complied with and reported to the concerned authority.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOrDetailsOfTheLawOrRegulat: Central Ground Water Authority (GWA), New Delhi
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideDetailsOfTheNonComplian: Non-compliance of No Objection Certificate conditions issued by CGWA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AnyFinesOrPenaltiesOrActionTak: CGWA has imposed penalty amount of ₹ 3,00,000/- (Rupees Three Lakhs Only)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CorrectiveActionTakenForNonCom: All compliances as required has been complied with and reported to the concerned authority.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideDetailsOfTheNonComplian: Violation of Maharashtra Pollution Control Board Regulations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AnyFinesOrPenaltiesOrActionTak: MPCB has imposed penal charges of ₹ 1,81,973/- (Rupees One Lakh Eighty-One Thousand Nine Hundred Seventy-Three Only).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: The Soyabean Processors Association of India (SOPA)
Soya Food Promotion and Welfare Association
The Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)
Indian Oil Seed & Produce Export Promotion Council (IOPEPC)
Oil Palm Developers and Processors Association
Round Table on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: We have established a robust mechanism to ensure prompt and effective resolution of consumer complaints and feedback. Consumers can lodge complaints related to our products through multiple accessible channels, including: Email: wecare@patanjalifoods.co.in Toll-free number: 1800 180 4109 Website: www.patanjalifoods.com/ContactUs.php Our dedicated Customer Care Cell engages directly with consumers to ensure swift redressal. All complaints are systematically recorded in our internal systems, and the status of redressal is regularly reviewed and escalated when necessary to ensure timely resolution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarkForConsumerComplaints: The company is in the process of resolving the pending complaints received in normal course in accordance with Grievance Redressal Mechanism of the company

All customer complaints were recorded, reviewed, and resolved within defined timelines, with feedback used to strengthen product and service standards. The outstanding complaints were closed at the last financial year
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ReasonsForForcedRecall: As per directives of the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI), we recalled a specific batch (Batch No. AJD2400012) of our red chilli powder. The recall, was undertaken as the batch did not meet the prescribed limits for pesticide residues under the Food Safety and Standards (Contaminants, Toxins and Residues) Regulations, 2011.
